title: Router vs Next.js
brief:
# Brief 5: Router vs Next.js vs React Router comparison

A flat-tech infographic side-by-side comparison of three routing solutions. English labels only.

Three vertical columns on a clean off-white background, each column has a header module and a stacked set of feature rows underneath.

Column 1 header: "TanStack Router / Start" with mint-green accent dot
Column 2 header: "React Router / Remix" with neutral gray dot
Column 3 header: "Next.js" with neutral gray dot

Feature rows (left-aligned labels in a fourth column), each row has small status indicators in the three columns:
- "Typesafe Routes" - column 1 fully green, others partial
- "Typesafe Search Params" - column 1 fully green, others red
- "Custom Param Parsing" - column 1 green, others red
- "Router Loaders + SWR Cache" - column 1 green, others mixed
- "History/Memory/Hash Routers" - columns 1 and 2 green, column 3 red
- "File-based Routing" - all three green
- "Code-based + Virtual Routing" - column 1 green, column 2 green, column 3 red
- "Generic RPC + Middleware" - column 1 green, others red
- "Parallel Routes" - columns 2 and 3 green, column 1 red
- "React Server Components" - column 3 green, others experimental

At the bottom of each column, a single subtle banner summarizing: Column 1 "Composable, headless, type-first", Column 2 "Mature, broad adoption", Column 3 "Integrated full-stack, Vercel-tuned".

Color: tanstack mint #32E6E2 highlights, soft slate gray neutrals, off-white background, very subtle drop shadows.

Style: flat-tech-infographic, clean, document-friendly, generous whitespace, no Chinese text.
prompt:

Make it suitable for insertion into engineering notes or a design document. Prefer a clear information hierarchy with section titles, concise Chinese/English labels, short notes, arrows, grouped modules, and strong readability. Avoid dense paragraph blocks.
